primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of foreign exchange exposure arising from intragroup dividend receivable for hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Directly governs hedge accounting eligibility, establishing that hedge accounting can only be applied to hedged items with parties external to the reporting entity at consolidated level, with a specific exception for certain intragroup monetary items with functional currency differences

references: not explicitly in chunks but referenced in IFRS 9 6.3.6
primary_authority.reason: Defines monetary vs non-monetary items and functional currency concepts that determine whether the dividend receivable qualifies for the intragroup exception

references: not explicitly in chunks but referenced in IFRS 9 6.3.5
supporting_authority.reason: Governs consolidation and elimination of intragroup transactions, referenced as basis for the investment entity exception to elimination
Governs related party transaction disclosures including intragroup transactions; while not directly on hedge eligibility, establishes the related party framework for intragroup dividends

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses net investment hedges but specifically excludes application to intragroup transactions by analogy (paragraph 8), not directly addressing dividend receivables
Addresses separate vs consolidated financial statements but does not establish hedge accounting eligibility criteria

treatment_families.family: Full elimination of intragroup foreign exchange exposure without hedge accounting eligibility
Limited eligibility for intragroup monetary items with functional currency differences

approaches.label: No hedge accounting for intragroup dividend receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 prohibits hedge accounting for hedged items arising from intragroup transactions in consolidated statements, as only external party transactions qualify; intragroup dividends are eliminated on consolidation and the resulting foreign exchange exposure cannot be designated as a hedged item at consolidated level unless a specific exception applies

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item foreign currency risk exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 provides a limited exception permitting hedge accounting for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items when functional currencies differ and the risk affects consolidated profit or loss; however, this exception applies to monetary items (such as loans or trade receivables) and not to non-monetary dividend receivables from equity investments, which are governed by IAS 21's classification of monetary items
